name: Union and Intersection Types
summary: Union types model alternatives; intersection types model simultaneous capabilities. Pair naturally with control-flow narrowing, pattern matching, and dynamic-language migration. TypeScript narrowing follows JavaScript control flow and type guards; Typed Racket uses occurrence typing with propositions attached to predicates.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Union types model alternatives; intersection types model simultaneous capabilities. They pair naturally with control-flow narrowing, pattern matching, and dynamic-language migration.

A union type `A | B` has values that are either `A` or `B`; an intersection type `A & B` has values that are *both* `A` and `B` simultaneously — useful for combining trait/interface obligations or for narrowing a value's static type after a runtime check.

sections.title: Production narrowing — pointer to occurrence typing
sections.content: TypeScript flow analysis and Typed Racket's occurrence typing are the two production approaches to narrowing union types based on runtime predicates and control flow. Full treatment of the discipline — including TypeScript's `typeof`/type-guard rules and Typed Racket's proposition language — lives in `data/occurrence-typing`.

sections.title: Design trade-offs
sections.content:
Union and intersection types blur the line between subtyping and type computation. Practical issues:

- **distributivity**: should `A | (B & C)` equal `(A | B) & (A | C)`? Yes if the lattice is distributive (MLsub assumes this); not all languages commit either way.
- **erased vs reified**: TypeScript erases unions at runtime, so a `string | number` value carries no tag — narrowing must be done by `typeof`. Languages with reified union tags (Scala 3 enums, Rust `enum`) get cheap pattern matching at the cost of explicit constructor calls.
- **soundness with mutation**: a value of type `A & B` stored in a mutable cell typed as `A` can have its `B` half invalidated by a write — most languages refuse intersection-typed mutable storage or require unique borrowing.

For language designers, unions are a strong tool for migration from dynamic code; full intersection types are rare in production but appear in TypeScript, Scala 3, and Java 8+ via type intersection in generic bounds.
